zh.lifeSummary: Roger Marmion於1035年之前與Gersande Marmion在艾歐洛結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Roger Marmion于1035年之前与Gersande Marmion在艾歐洛结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Marmion
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: perhaps an Anglicized form of an unidentified Gaelic name. Compare   Merriman  .
English (Lancashire; of Norman origin) and French: nickname from Old French marmion ‘monkey, brat’.

variants.name: Merriman
meanings: nickname from Middle English mirie, merie, murie ‘merry, pleasant’ + man ‘man’. Compare Merry 1. This surname is established in Ireland (perhaps as an Anglicized form of an unidentified Gaelic name; compare Marmion ) as well as England.

there has probably been some confusion with Marriman, probably an occupational name meaning ‘servant of Mary’, from the female personal name Mary (see Marie ) + Middle English man in the sense ‘servant’, or perhaps a pet form of Mary using the hypocoristic suffix -man. The surname Marriman is now vanishingly rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Marmon
meanings: variant of Marriman, an occupational name meaning ‘servant of Mary’, from the female personal name Mary (see Marie ) + Middle English man in the sense ‘servant’.

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: Roger Marmion married Gersande Marmion before 1035, in Europe. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Roger Marmion a épousé Gersande Marmion avant 1035, à Europe. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Roger Marmion hat vor 1035 in Europa Gersande Marmion geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Roger Marmion lui sposato Gersande Marmion prima del 1035, nel Europa. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Roger Marmionは1035年以前にヨーロッパでGersande Marmionと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Roger Marmion(은)는 1035년 이전 우럽에서 Gersande Marmion(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Roger Marmion casou-se com Gersande Marmion antes 1035, em Europa. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Roger Marmion женился на женщине по имени Gersande Marmion перед 1035 г. в местности Европа. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Roger Marmion se casó con Gersande Marmion antes de 1035, en Europa. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
